channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Backup QB Arbuckle leads Toronto past Winnipeg 41-24 for the Argonauts' 19th Grey Cup title
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Backup quarterback Nick Arbuckle led the Toronto Argonauts to their record-extending 19th Grey Cup title, throwing two second-half touchdown passes in a 41-24 victory over the Winnipeg Blue Bombers on Sunday night at B.C. Place.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cunningham's 4th triple-double of the season lifts the Pistons to a 124-104 rout of Washington
item.description: Cade Cunningham had 21 points, 10 assists and 10 rebounds to lead the Detroit Pistons over the Washington Wizards 124-104.
item.dc:creator: Noah Trister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Josh Allen's 26-yard run seals Bills' 30-21 win over KC and ends Chiefs' bid for a perfect season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — After watching quarterback Josh Allen bounce off two Kansas City Chiefs defenders before crossing the goal line on a 26-yard run, Bills receiver Khalil Shakir joined the rest of the packed Buffalo stadium by screaming at the top of his lungs.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: James Harden breaks tie with Ray Allen, moves into No. 2 on NBA's career 3-point list
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — James Harden now stands alone at No. 2 in 3-pointers. Harden, the Los Angeles Clippers guard, connected on the 2,974th 3-pointer of his career on Sunday night and broke a tie with Basketball Hall of Famer Ray Allen for the No. 2 spot on the NBA's list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Darius Garland scores 25, Cavaliers become 4th team to start 15-0 with 128-114 win over Hornets
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Darius Garland scored 25 points, Ty Jerome matched his career-high with 24 in place of Donovan Mitchell and the Cleveland Cavaliers stayed unbeaten with a 128-114 win over the Charlotte Hornets on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Dallas Wings win the WNBA draft lottery, earning the No. 1 pick next year
item.description: The future of the Dallas Wings got a lot brighter as the franchise won the WNBA draft lottery on Sunday and with it the chance to draft UConn star Paige Bueckers with the No. 1 pick. “We know what No.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: Kris Byrant's Lamborghini Huracan stolen from Colorado home
item.description: Kris Bryant's Lamborghini Huracan was stolen by a multi-state vehicle theft ring, but was recovered by police in Las Vegas after they traced the stolen vehicle to a home in the city.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Packers block game-winning field goal to beat Bears, pick up 11th straight victory over rival
item.description: The Green Bay Packers blocked a game-winning field goal attempt by the Chicago Bears to defeat their NFC North rival, 20-19, in a thrilling battle at Soldier Field.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Steelers take command of AFC North with win over Ravens
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers hung on for a two-point win against their division rival the Baltimore Ravens on Sunday, 18-16. The Steelers are clearly in first place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Richardson's late TD run leads Colts to a 28-27 victory over Rodgers and the Jets
item.description: Anthony Richardson ran for a 4-yard touchdown, his second of the game, with 46 seconds left in his return to the starting role to lift the Indianapolis Colts to a 28-27 victory over Aaron Rodgers and the New York Jets.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Waszak Jr., Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump thanks Dana White, UFC fans for raucous welcome at MSG
item.description: President-elect Trump thanked UFC president Dana White and fight fans for the hospitality he received at Madison Square Garden on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump presidency 'positive for the world,' UFC president Dana White says
item.description: Jon Jones and other fighters came out in support of President-elect Trump on Saturday night as he was seated cage-side next to Dana White and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jon 'Bones' Jones performs Donald Trump's famous dance, gives title belt to president-elect after TKO victory
item.description: Jon "Bones" Jones retained his heavyweight title belt after defeating Stipe Miocic by TKO, and to celebrate, he pointed to President-elect Donald Trump and did his signature dance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LeBron James puts Deion Sanders' critics on notice, says 'haters' are 'in hiding' after Colorado's latest win
item.description: Colorado was one of the most intriguing teams in college football last season, but they were not able to put together very many wins. The 2024 season has been much different.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LSU coach Brian Kelly screams at one player and gets yelled at by another in loss at Florida
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — LSU coach Brian Kelly was caught on camera screaming at one player and getting yelled at by another. The sideline scenes were clear signs of frustration for a program that was on its way to losing a third consecutive game, 27-16 at unranked Florida on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah State crushes Hawaii, 55-10
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Rahsul Faison rushed for 192 yards and two touchdowns, as the Utah State football team cruised past Hawaii Saturday at Maverik Stadium, 55-10.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Netflix experiences streaming delays leading up to Tyson-Paul fight
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Netflix's first attempt at handling a live sports event did not receive a passing grade. The fight Friday night between Mike Tyson and Jake Paul experienced streaming problems according to many viewers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Draymond Green's foul against Zach Edey is upgraded by the NBA to a flagrant 1
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green's foul against Memphis' Zach Edey in the third quarter of the Warriors' victory over the Grizzlies has been upgraded to a category-one flagrant, the NBA said Saturday after reviewing the play.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah HC falls at home to Vegas, 4-2
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - William Karlsson scored the go-ahead goal with 1:18 remaining, and the Vegas Golden Knights rallied for a 4-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Delta Center on Friday. “We stuck with it and eventually we got rewarded,” Karlsson said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Nuggets star Nikola Jokic and coach Michael Malone miss game against Pelicans for personal reasons
item.description: The Denver Nuggets were without star center Nikola Jokic and coach Michael Malone for their NBA Cup opener Friday night against the New Orleans Pelicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bird, Fowles, Pondexter, Beard headline Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025
item.description: WNBA greats Sue Bird, Sylvia Fowles, Cappie Pondexter and Alana Beard headline the Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025 that was announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Roy High to play for first state title since 1981
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Roy High football team will play for its first state championship in 43 years. Roy defeated defending 5A champion Timpview on Friday, 29-19 to advance to next week's title game at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Watch out MLB! Keep an eye out for Roki Sasaki
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- As all the attention in baseball is focused on Juan Soto, Scott Boras, and his free agency, there is another that should draw attention as well. That is Japanese righty Roki Sasaki. The 23-year-old really flashed in the World Baseball Classic a couple of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays to play 2025 season at Yankees' spring training field
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rays will play their 2025 home games at the New York Yankees' nearby spring training ballpark amid uncertainty about the future of hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field, Rays executives told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Olympic champion Lindsey Vonn is ending her retirement at age 40 to make a skiing comeback
item.description: Vonn's last competition came in February 2019, after she dealt with a long series of injuries throughout her career.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bears release OL Nate Davis
item.description: CHICAGO — The Nate Davis era is over at Halas Hall after the Bears cut him Wednesday. The lineman had a tough time finding the field despite being paid over $10 million a year.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Interim Oakland Roots head coach gets promoted
item.description: (KRON) – Oakland Roots Soccer Club Interim Head Coach Gavin Glinton can finally ditch the “interim” of his title. The soccer club announced that Glinton has been promoted to full-time Head Coach ahead of the 2025 United Soccer League Championship season.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Dodgers fan claims he was fired for calling in sick, attending World Series parade
item.description: People are willing to go to great lengths to support their beloved sports teams—one person even lost his job for it. A Los Angeles Dodgers fan claims he was fired after being spotted at the team's World Series victory parade after calling in sick for work.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5
